metadata.description: Malaysia K-12 Sejarah (History) chapters with prerequisite ordering, colored by textbook

nodes.label: Knowing History
nodes.description: Introduction to history — Pengertian Sejarah, sources of history, research methods, space and time, cause and effect in historical events

nodes.label: Me and My Family
nodes.description: Personal and family history — Saya dan Keluarga, understanding one's own heritage and family lineage

nodes.label: History of School
nodes.description: History of one's school — Sejarah Sekolah, understanding the origins and development of educational institutions

nodes.label: History of Place of Residence
nodes.description: History of one's local area — Sejarah Tempat Tinggal, understanding the historical development of one's community

nodes.label: Tokoh Terbilang Tempatan
nodes.description: Local distinguished figures — Tokoh Terbilang Tempatan, learning about notable personalities from the local community and their contributions

nodes.label: Kesultanan Melayu Melaka
nodes.description: The Malacca Sultanate — Kesultanan Melayu Melaka, its founding by Parameswara, administration, the golden age of Melaka, and the fall of the sultanate

nodes.label: Tokoh-tokoh Terbilang Melaka
nodes.description: Distinguished figures of Melaka — Tokoh-tokoh Terbilang, Hang Tuah the legendary warrior and Tun Perak the great Bendahara of the Malacca Sultanate

nodes.label: Warisan Kesultanan Melayu Melaka
nodes.description: Heritage of the Malacca Sultanate — Warisan Kesultanan Melayu Melaka, the cultural, administrative, and legal legacy of the sultanate

nodes.label: Peranan Melaka sebagai Pusat Perdagangan
nodes.description: Melaka as a trading hub — Peranan Melaka sebagai Pusat Perdagangan, the role of Melaka as the premier entrepot and its international trade networks

nodes.label: The Royal Institution
nodes.description: The institution of the Malay rulers — Institusi Raja Payung Negara, the role and significance of the monarchy in Malaysia

nodes.label: Islam in Malaysia
nodes.description: The spread and role of Islam — Agama Islam di Malaysia, the arrival of Islam and its influence on Malay society and governance

nodes.label: Malay Language Our Heritage
nodes.description: The Malay language as cultural heritage — Bahasa Melayu Warisan Kita, the development and significance of Bahasa Melayu

nodes.label: Formation of Malaysia
nodes.description: The birth of Malaysia — Pembentukan Malaysia, the merger of Malaya, Sabah, Sarawak, and Singapore in 1963

nodes.label: States of Malaysia
nodes.description: The states and territories — Negeri-negeri di Malaysia, an overview of the thirteen states and federal territories

nodes.label: Penjajahan Kuasa Asing
nodes.description: Colonization by foreign powers — Penjajahan Kuasa Asing, an overview of Portuguese, Dutch, British, and Japanese colonization of Tanah Melayu

nodes.label: National Identity Symbols
nodes.description: Symbols of national identity — Jalur Gemilang, Lagu Kebangsaan (Negaraku), Jata Negara, and Rukun Negara as the foundations of Malaysian nationhood

nodes.label: Ethnic Groups in Malaysia
nodes.description: Malaysia's diverse communities — Kaum Di Malaysia, the major ethnic groups and their contributions to nation-building

nodes.label: Religion and Beliefs in Malaysia
nodes.description: Religious diversity — Agama dan Kepercayaan di Malaysia, the role of Islam as the official religion and the practice of other faiths

nodes.label: Community Celebrations in Malaysia
nodes.description: Festivals and celebrations — Perayaan Masyarakat Di Malaysia, the cultural festivities of Malaysia's diverse communities

nodes.label: Leaders of Malaysia
nodes.description: National leaders — Pemimpin Negara Malaysia, the prime ministers and key political figures who shaped modern Malaysia

nodes.label: Perpaduan Kaum dan Integrasi Nasional
nodes.description: Racial unity and national integration — Perpaduan Kaum dan Integrasi Nasional, efforts towards building a united Malaysian society across ethnic lines

nodes.label: Knowing History (Form 1)
nodes.description: Deeper study of historical methodology — Mengenali Sejarah, definitions, sources, research methods, interpretation, and the importance of history

nodes.label: The Ice Age (Form 1)
nodes.description: In-depth study of the Ice Age — Zaman Air Batu, geological changes, land bridges, and their impact on Southeast Asia

nodes.label: Prehistoric Era (Form 1)
nodes.description: In-depth study of prehistory — Zaman Prasejarah, Paleolithic, Mesolithic, Neolithic periods and early human migration

nodes.label: Knowing Civilizations
nodes.description: Introduction to world civilizations — Mengenali Tamadun, the concept and characteristics of civilization

nodes.label: Early World Civilizations
nodes.description: Ancient civilizations — Tamadun Awal Dunia, Mesopotamia, Egypt, Indus Valley, and Huang He civilizations

nodes.label: Rise of Greek-Roman Civilization
nodes.description: Classical civilizations — Peningkatan Tamadun Yunani Rom, contributions of Greece and Rome to governance, philosophy, and culture

nodes.label: Rise of Indian and Chinese Civilization
nodes.description: Asian civilizations — Peningkatan Tamadun India dan China, contributions and influence on Southeast Asia

nodes.label: Islamic Civilization and Contributions
nodes.description: Islamic civilization — Tamadun Islam dan Sumbangan, the rise of Islam and its contributions to science, art, and governance

nodes.label: Tamadun Awal di Asia Tenggara
nodes.description: Early civilizations in Southeast Asia — Tamadun Awal di Asia Tenggara, the development of early kingdoms and cultural influences in the region

nodes.label: Kingdoms of the Malay World
nodes.description: Early Malay kingdoms — Kerajaan Alam Melayu, Srivijaya, Majapahit, and other maritime and land-based kingdoms

nodes.label: Governance and Economy
nodes.description: Political and economic systems — Sistem Pemerintahan dan Kegiatan Ekonomi, governance structures and trade in the Malay world

nodes.label: Socio-culture of Society
nodes.description: Social and cultural life — Sosiobudaya Masyarakat, customs, traditions, and social structures of Malay world communities

nodes.label: Religion, Beliefs and Heritage
nodes.description: Religious influence and cultural heritage — Agama, Kepercayaan dan Keunikan Warisan, Hinduism, Buddhism, Islam and their legacy

nodes.label: Pengislaman Alam Melayu
nodes.description: Islamization of the Malay World — Pengislaman Alam Melayu, the spread of Islam through trade, missionaries, and its impact on Malay society and governance

nodes.label: The Malacca Sultanate (Form 2)
nodes.description: In-depth study of Malacca — Kesultanan Melayu Melaka, administration, trade, society, and the golden age of the sultanate

nodes.label: The Johor-Riau Sultanate
nodes.description: Successor to Malacca — Kesultanan Johor Riau, the continuation of Malay sovereignty after the fall of Malacca

nodes.label: Sultanates of Pahang, Perak, Terengganu, and Selangor
nodes.description: Malay sultanates — Kesultanan Melayu Pahang, Perak, Terengganu, dan Selangor, their establishment and governance

nodes.label: Kedah, Kelantan, Negeri Sembilan, and Perlis
nodes.description: Northern and central states — Kerajaan Kedah, Kelantan, Negeri Sembilan, dan Perlis, their unique governance systems

nodes.label: Heritage of Kedah, Kelantan, Negeri Sembilan, and Perlis
nodes.description: Cultural heritage — Warisan Kerajaan Kedah, Kelantan, Negeri Sembilan dan Perlis, unique traditions and legacy

nodes.label: Sarawak and Sabah
nodes.description: East Malaysian states — Sarawak dan Sabah, the history, governance, and unique heritage of Borneo territories

nodes.label: Hubungan dengan Kuasa Luar
nodes.description: Relations with external powers — Hubungan dengan Kuasa Luar, diplomatic and trade relations between the Malay world and foreign powers including China, India, and the Arab world

nodes.label: Arrival of Western Powers
nodes.description: European colonialism begins — Kedatangan Kuasa Barat, the Portuguese, Dutch, and British arrival in the Malay world

nodes.label: Administration of Straits Settlements
nodes.description: British Straits Settlements — Pentadbiran Negeri-negeri Selat, Penang, Malacca, and Singapore under British rule

nodes.label: Perjanjian-perjanjian
nodes.description: Treaties and agreements — Perjanjian-perjanjian, key treaties such as the Pangkor Treaty 1874 and their impact on Malay sovereignty

nodes.label: Federated Malay States Administration
nodes.description: The FMS — Pentadbiran Negeri-negeri Melayu Bersekutu, British Resident system in Perak, Selangor, Pahang, and Negeri Sembilan

nodes.label: Unfederated Malay States Administration
nodes.description: The UMS — Pentadbiran Negeri-negeri Melayu Tidak Bersekutu, British influence in Kedah, Kelantan, Terengganu, Perlis, and Johor

nodes.label: Western Administration in Sarawak and Sabah
nodes.description: Colonial rule in Borneo — Pentadbiran Barat di Sarawak dan Sabah, the Brooke dynasty and North Borneo Company

nodes.label: Perkembangan Ekonomi Zaman Penjajahan
nodes.description: Economic development during colonization — Perkembangan Ekonomi Zaman Penjajahan, tin mining, rubber plantations, immigration policies, and economic exploitation

nodes.label: Sistem Pendidikan Zaman Penjajahan
nodes.description: Education system during colonization — Sistem Pendidikan Zaman Penjajahan, vernacular schools, English schools, and the impact on different ethnic communities

nodes.label: Impact of Western Administration
nodes.description: Colonial consequences — Kesan Pentadbiran Barat, economic exploitation, social changes, and political impact

nodes.label: Local Resistance
nodes.description: Anti-colonial struggles — Penentangan Masyarakat Tempatan, resistance by local communities against Western powers

nodes.label: Wisdom of Malay Rulers and Chiefs
nodes.description: Malay leadership — Kebijaksanaan Raja dan Pembesar Melayu, diplomatic strategies of rulers in dealing with colonial powers

nodes.label: Heritage of the Nation
nodes.description: National heritage foundations — Warisan Negara Bangsa, the historical roots and identity of the Malaysian nation

nodes.label: Rise of Nationalism
nodes.description: Malay nationalism — Kebangkitan Nasionalisme, the growth of national consciousness and political movements

nodes.label: Nasionalisme Kaum Lain
nodes.description: Nationalism of other communities — Nasionalisme Kaum Lain, Chinese and Indian nationalist movements in Malaya and their role in the independence struggle

nodes.label: World Conflicts and Japanese Occupation
nodes.description: WWII in Malaya — Konflik Dunia dan Pendudukan Jepun, the impact of Japanese occupation from 1941 to 1945

nodes.label: British Power Transition Era
nodes.description: Post-war British return — Era Peralihan Kuasa British, the Malayan Union proposal and Malay opposition

nodes.label: Penubuhan UMNO
nodes.description: Founding of UMNO — Penubuhan UMNO, the establishment of the United Malays National Organisation in 1946 as a response to the Malayan Union

nodes.label: Federation of Malaya 1948
nodes.description: The Federation agreement — Persekutuan Tanah Melayu 1948, replacing the Malayan Union with a new constitutional framework

nodes.label: Communist Threat and Emergency
nodes.description: The Malayan Emergency — Ancaman Komunis dan Perisytiharan Darurat, the communist insurgency and the state of emergency

nodes.label: Efforts Towards Independence
nodes.description: The road to Merdeka — Usaha Ke Arah Kemerdekaan, political negotiations, the Reid Commission, and constitutional talks

nodes.label: Suruhanjaya Reid
nodes.description: The Reid Commission — Suruhanjaya Reid, the constitutional commission that drafted the Federation of Malaya's independence constitution

nodes.label: Elections
nodes.description: Early democratic processes — Pilihan Raya, the first municipal and federal elections leading to self-governance

nodes.label: Federal Constitution 1957
nodes.description: The Merdeka constitution — Perlembagaan Persekutuan Tanah Melayu 1957, drafting and key provisions of the constitution

nodes.label: Proclamation of Independence
nodes.description: Merdeka 1957 — Pemasyhuran Kemerdekaan, the declaration of independence on 31 August 1957 by Tunku Abdul Rahman

nodes.label: National Sovereignty
nodes.description: Sovereignty of the nation — Kedaulatan Negara, the concept and importance of national sovereignty in the Malaysian context

nodes.label: Federal Constitution
nodes.description: The supreme law — Perlembagaan Persekutuan, detailed study of the Malaysian Federal Constitution and its amendments

nodes.label: Constitutional Monarchy and Parliamentary Democracy
nodes.description: System of governance — Raja Berperlembagaan dan Demokrasi Berparlimen, the dual pillars of Malaysian governance

nodes.label: Federal System
nodes.description: Federalism in Malaysia — Sistem Persekutuan, the division of powers between federal and state governments

nodes.label: Formation of Malaysia (Form 5)
nodes.description: In-depth study of Malaysia's formation — Pembentukan Malaysia, the political process, negotiations, and the Malaysia Agreement 1963

nodes.label: Post-Malaysia Formation Challenges
nodes.description: Challenges after 1963 — Cabaran Selepas Pembentukan Malaysia, Konfrontasi, Singapore's separation, and early nation-building difficulties

nodes.label: Peristiwa 13 Mei 1969 dan Rukun Negara
nodes.description: The May 13 Incident and Rukun Negara — Peristiwa 13 Mei 1969, the racial riots and the subsequent formulation of the Rukun Negara as a national ideology

nodes.label: Dasar Ekonomi Baru (NEP)
nodes.description: New Economic Policy — Dasar Ekonomi Baru, the NEP introduced in 1971 to eradicate poverty and restructure society regardless of race

nodes.label: Building National Well-being
nodes.description: Social development — Membina Kesejahteraan Negara, national unity policies, education, and social welfare programs

nodes.label: Building National Prosperity
nodes.description: Economic policies — Membina Kemakmuran Negara, NDP, Vision 2020, WKB2030, and Malaysia's economic transformation

nodes.label: Wawasan 2020 dan WKB2030
nodes.description: Vision 2020 and Shared Prosperity Vision 2030 — Wawasan 2020 dan WKB2030, national development visions for a developed and inclusive Malaysia

nodes.label: Perpaduan Kaum
nodes.description: Racial unity — Perpaduan Kaum, efforts and policies to foster inter-ethnic harmony and national integration in Malaysia

nodes.label: Malaysia's Foreign Policy
nodes.description: International relations — Dasar Luar Malaysia, Malaysia's foreign policy principles and involvement in international organizations

nodes.label: Malaysia's World Excellence
nodes.description: Global achievements — Kecemerlangan Malaysia di Persada Dunia, Malaysia's contributions and recognition on the world stage

nodes.label: Society in World History
nodes.description: STPM World History theme — Social structures, class systems, and community organisation across major world civilisations

nodes.label: Government in World History
nodes.description: STPM World History theme — Political systems, governance models, empires, and the evolution of state authority across civilisations

nodes.label: Progress in World History
nodes.description: STPM World History theme — Economic development, industrialisation, technological advancement, and modernisation in world history

nodes.label: Nationalism in World History
nodes.description: STPM World History theme — Rise of nationalism, independence movements, revolutions, and nation-building across the world

nodes.label: Industrial Revolution
nodes.description: The Industrial Revolution — origins in Britain, mechanisation, factory system, urbanisation, and its global economic and social impact

nodes.label: French Revolution
nodes.description: The French Revolution — causes, key events (1789–1799), the fall of the monarchy, the Reign of Terror, and its impact on modern political thought

nodes.label: American Revolution
nodes.description: The American Revolution — colonial grievances, the Declaration of Independence, the Revolutionary War, and the founding of the United States

nodes.label: World War I
nodes.description: World War I — causes (alliances, imperialism, nationalism), major events, the Treaty of Versailles, and its consequences for the world order

nodes.label: World War II
nodes.description: World War II — causes, the rise of fascism, major theatres of war, the Holocaust, atomic bombings, and the post-war settlement

nodes.label: Cold War
nodes.description: The Cold War — US-Soviet rivalry, the Iron Curtain, proxy wars, the arms race, and the eventual collapse of the Soviet Union

nodes.label: Decolonization
nodes.description: Decolonization — the dismantling of colonial empires in Asia, Africa, and the Caribbean, independence movements, and the emergence of new nations

nodes.label: Society and Government in Islamic History
nodes.description: STPM Islamic History theme — Social structures and governance in Islamic civilisation, the caliphate system, and political thought

nodes.label: Development in Islamic History
nodes.description: STPM Islamic History theme — Intellectual, scientific, economic, and cultural achievements of Islamic civilisation

nodes.label: Nationalism in Islamic History
nodes.description: STPM Islamic History theme — Nationalist movements in the Muslim world, anti-colonial struggles, and modern nation-state formation

nodes.label: Zaman Jahiliyyah
nodes.description: The Age of Ignorance — Zaman Jahiliyyah, pre-Islamic Arabian society, customs, beliefs, and social conditions before the advent of Islam

nodes.label: Sirah Rasulullah
nodes.description: Life of the Prophet — Sirah Rasulullah, the biography of Prophet Muhammad SAW, from birth to the establishment of the Islamic state in Madinah

nodes.label: Khulafa al-Rashidin
nodes.description: The Rightly Guided Caliphs — Khulafa al-Rashidin, Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, and Ali, their governance and contributions to Islamic civilisation

nodes.label: Bani Umayyah
nodes.description: The Umayyad Dynasty — Bani Umayyah, the expansion of the Islamic empire, administration, and cultural developments under Umayyad rule

nodes.label: Bani Abbasiyyah
nodes.description: The Abbasid Dynasty — Bani Abbasiyyah, the golden age of Islam, intellectual achievements, and the House of Wisdom in Baghdad

nodes.label: Kerajaan Uthmaniyyah
nodes.description: The Ottoman Empire — Kerajaan Uthmaniyyah, its rise, expansion, governance, cultural contributions, and eventual decline

nodes.label: Gerakan Islah
nodes.description: Islamic reform movements — Gerakan Islah, reformist thinkers and movements aimed at revitalising the Muslim world in the modern era

nodes.label: Local Community in MY & SEA History
nodes.description: STPM MY & SEA History theme — Indigenous communities, local social structures, and community life in Malaysia and Southeast Asia

nodes.label: Transformation in MY & SEA History
nodes.description: STPM MY & SEA History theme — Political, economic, and social transformation in Malaysia and Southeast Asia through colonial and modern eras

nodes.label: Nationalism in MY & SEA History
nodes.description: STPM MY & SEA History theme — Nationalist movements, political awakening, and anti-colonial resistance in Malaysia and Southeast Asia

nodes.label: Independence in MY & SEA History
nodes.description: STPM MY & SEA History theme — Independence movements, nation-building, and post-independence development in Malaysia and Southeast Asia

nodes.label: Kerajaan Melayu Awal
nodes.description: Early Malay kingdoms — Kerajaan Melayu Awal, Langkasuka, Srivijaya, Majapahit, and other early states in the Malay Archipelago

nodes.label: Penjajahan Barat di Asia Tenggara
nodes.description: Western colonization in Southeast Asia — Penjajahan Barat di Asia Tenggara, Portuguese, Spanish, Dutch, British, and French colonialism across the region

nodes.label: Nasionalisme di Negara SEA
nodes.description: Nationalism in Southeast Asian nations — Nasionalisme di Negara-negara Asia Tenggara, independence movements in Indonesia, Philippines, Vietnam, Myanmar, and others

nodes.label: Pembinaan Negara-bangsa
nodes.description: Nation-state building — Pembinaan Negara-bangsa, the challenges and processes of building nation-states in Malaysia and Southeast Asia after independence
